lede: A look at January 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Eminem and Gustave Doré, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Donald Trump
events.desc: Supporters of U.S. president Donald Trump storm the United States Capitol Building to disrupt certification of the 2020 presidential election, resulting in four deaths and evacuation of the U.S. Congress.

events.title: Manila
events.desc: A chemical fire in an apartment complex in Manila, Philippines, leads to the discovery of plans for Project Bojinka, a mass-terrorist attack.

events.title: National Airlines Flight 2511
events.desc: National Airlines Flight 2511 is destroyed in mid-air by a bomb, while en route from New York City to Miami.

events.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
events.desc: United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt delivers his Four Freedoms speech in the State of the Union address.

events.title: New Mexico
events.desc: New Mexico is admitted to the Union as the 47th U.S. state.

events.title: Night of the Big Wind
events.desc: The Night of the Big Wind, the most damaging storm in 300 years, sweeps across Ireland, damaging or destroying more than 20% of the houses in Dublin.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Liebster Immanuel, Herzog der Frommen, BWV 123, a chorale cantata for Epiphany.

events.title: Henry VIII
events.desc: King Henry VIII of England marries Anne of Cleves.

events.title: Colegio de Santa Cruz de Tlatelolco
events.desc: The first European school of higher learning in the Americas, Colegio de Santa Cruz de Tlatelolco, is founded by Viceroy Antonio de Mendoza and Bishop Juan de Zumárraga in Mexico City.

events.title: Stefan Dečanski
events.desc: Stephen Uroš III is crowned King of Serbia, having defeated his half-brother Stefan Konstantin in battle. His son is crowned "young king" in the same ceremony.

births.name: José Gaspar Rodríguez de Francia
births.line: Paraguayan lawyer and politician, first dictator of Paraguay

deaths.text: Dizzy Gillespie, American singer-songwriter and trumpet player
Victor Fleming, American director, producer, and cinematographer
Jakob Rosanes, German mathematician and chess player
Theodore Roosevelt, American colonel and politician, 26th President of the United States
Christopher of Bavaria, King of Denmark, Norway and Sweden

observances.text: Christian Feast day: André Bessette (Roman Catholic Church)
Epiphany or Three Kings' Day (Western Christianity) or Theophany (Eastern Christianity), and its related observances: Little Christmas (Ireland)
Epiphany or Three Kings' Day (Western Christianity) or Theophany (Eastern Christianity), and its related observances: Þrettándinn (Iceland)
